front: What is the Bolzano-Weierstrass property in a metric space?
back: A metric space (X, d) has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property if every infinite subset of X has a limit point in X.

front: How is the Bolzano-Weierstrass property related to sequential compactness?
back: For metric spaces, the Bolzano-Weierstrass property is equivalent to sequential compactness (every sequence has a convergent subsequence).

front: State the classical Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem for the real numbers.
back: Every bounded sequence in ℝ has a convergent subsequence. This is a specific instance of the property applied to ℝ with the usual metric.

front: True or False: In a general metric space, compactness is equivalent to the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
back: True. For metric spaces, the following are equivalent: 1) Compactness, 2) Sequential compactness, 3) The Bolzano-Weierstrass property.

front: Give an example of a metric space that does NOT have the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
back: The set of rational numbers ℚ with the standard metric. The infinite set {1, 1.4, 1.41, 1.414, ...} (approximations of √2) is contained in ℚ but has no limit point in ℚ.
